description: An in-range but unrecognized persistence value produces an unknown result without disclosing the raw value; the other codex rules' keys are absent and are separately unknown as unset.

findings.message: Cannot determine usage analytics telemetry posture: analytics.enabled is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented default sends anonymous usage and health metrics unless disabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.
findings.unknown_reason: analytics.enabled is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented default sends anonymous usage and health metrics unless disabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.

findings.message: Cannot determine feedback submission channel posture: feedback.enabled is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented default (true) leaves the /feedback submission channel enabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.
findings.unknown_reason: feedback.enabled is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented default (true) leaves the /feedback submission channel enabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.

findings.message: Cannot determine history persistence posture: history.persistence is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed
findings.unknown_reason: history.persistence is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed

findings.message: Cannot determine OpenTelemetry raw prompt export posture: otel.log_user_prompt is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented example redacts raw prompts from OpenTelemetry logs unless explicitly enabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.
findings.unknown_reason: otel.log_user_prompt is unset in the user-level config; Codex's documented example redacts raw prompts from OpenTelemetry logs unless explicitly enabled, but uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.
